name.es: Johnnycake de Rhode Island (torta de maíz)
name.fr: Johnnycake de Rhode Island (galette de maïs)

description.en: A pan-fried cornmeal cake older than the United States itself: white flint cornmeal is scalded into a thick mush, rested, then fried in fat until crisp outside and soft within. No flour, no leavening, no yeast.
description.de: Ein in der Pfanne gebratener Maiskuchen, älter als die USA selbst: weißes Maismehl wird zu einem dicken Brei aufgekocht, ruht kurz und wird dann in Fett gebraten, bis er außen knusprig und innen weich ist. Kein Weizenmehl, kein Triebmittel, keine Hefe.
description.es: Una torta de maíz frita en sartén, más antigua que Estados Unidos: la harina de maíz blanco se escalda hasta formar una papilla espesa, reposa y luego se fríe en grasa hasta quedar crujiente por fuera y suave por dentro. Sin harina de trigo, sin leudante, sin levadura.
description.fr: Une galette de maïs cuite à la poêle, plus ancienne que les États-Unis eux-mêmes : la farine de maïs blanc est ébouillantée en une bouillie épaisse, repose, puis frit dans la graisse jusqu'à devenir croustillante dehors et tendre dedans. Ni farine de blé, ni levure, ni levain.

origin.region: Rhode Island and southern New England
origin.note: Originally called 'journey cakes' for how well they traveled and cooked quickly over a campfire; Rhode Island's version uses a distinctive fine white flint corn, still stone-ground at local grist mills.

keeping.note: Best hot off the griddle; these don't keep well and are meant to be eaten fresh, though extra batter keeps a day refrigerated.

method.title: Make the mush
method.summary: Whisk the cornmeal, salt and sugar into boiling water off the heat until smooth, then stir in the butter.

method.title: Rest the batter
method.summary: Let the mixture rest about 15 minutes until it thickens to a soft, mashed-potato consistency.

method.title: Heat the pan
method.summary: Heat a well-greased cast-iron or heavy pan over medium-high heat.

method.title: Drop & flatten
method.summary: Drop quarter-cup scoops onto the hot pan, spacing them apart, and gently flatten each to about 1/4 inch thick.

method.title: Fry both sides
method.summary: Fry undisturbed until the edges brown, 6-12 minutes, then flip and fry the other side until golden and crisp.

method.title: Keep warm
method.summary: Transfer to a rack in a warm oven while frying the rest, then serve hot.

source.adaptation: Frying fat quantity estimated since the source leaves the amount to the cook's discretion.
